sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचत्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा । सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु ॥
hindiTranslation: श्रीभगवान् बोले  मनुष्योंकी वह स्वभावसे उत्पन्न हुई श्रद्धा सात्त्विकी तथा राजसी और तामसी  ऐसे तीन तरहकी ही होती है, उसको तुम मेरेसे सुनो ॥
englishTranslation: The faith of embodied beings is born of their nature. It is of three kinds: sattvic, rajasic, and tamasic. Listen to this.
essence: Faith takes the shape of the nature beneath it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Faith is not all the same. It comes from a person's nature and appears in three forms: clear, restless, or dull.
simpleInsight: Your deepest beliefs are not random; they reveal the quality of your inner state.
detailedMeaning: Arjuna has asked why people worship in different ways. On the battlefield at Kurukshetra, Krishna begins his answer by explaining that faith itself reflects a person's nature, and that it can be clear, restless, or dull.
modernRelevance: You watch someone defend a choice they barely understand, then realise the energy behind it matters as much as the belief itself. The shape of faith reveals the state underneath.
translationLiteral: त्रिविधा (threefold) / भवति (is) / श्रद्धा (faith) / देहिनां (of embodied beings) / सा (that) / स्वभावजा (born of nature) / सात्त्विकी (sattvic) / राजसी (rajasic) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / तामसी (tamasic) / चेति (thus) / तां (that) / श्रृणु (listen)
insight: It shifts attention from judging beliefs alone to noticing the inner quality that produces them.
